title: AWS tag keys should follow a naming convention
description: AWS tag keys should comply with AWS format requirements. Tag keys must be between 1 and 128 characters long and can only contain Unicode letters, digits, whitespace, and these special characters: _ . : / = + - @. Other special characters like <, >, {, }, \, |, `, ^, quotes are not allowed.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: 2010-09-09
Resources:
  MyInstance:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      Tags:
        - Key: "invalid<key"  # Non-compliant: contains < character
          Value: prod
        - Key: "another|key"  # Non-compliant: contains | character
          Value: john
        - Key: ""  # Non-compliant: empty key
          Value: test
compliant:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: 2010-09-09
Resources:
  MyInstance:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      Tags:
        - Key: "environment"  # Compliant: valid characters
          Value: prod
        - Key: "owner-name"  # Compliant: hyphen allowed
          Value: john
        - Key: "team@company"  # Compliant: @ symbol allowed
          Value: devops
        - Key: "cost:center"  # Compliant: colon allowed
          Value: finance

naming_convention.description: Tag keys should be 1-128 characters and only contain Unicode letters, digits, whitespace, and these characters: _ . : / = + - @
